notes: ICRC reported that the population of Al Obeid is facing mounting hardship following escalation of hostilities, with months of displacement, uncertainty and repeated disruptions to essential services including safe water, healthcare and other basic needs.

location: Al Obeid and displacement sites in North Kordofan
notes: ICRC reported that 42,000 people were assisted with a month's worth of food across camps and urban settlements in Al Obeid; nutritional supplements distributed to over 7,000 children and pregnant and lactating women.

ICRC reported that more than 850 weapon-wounded patients were treated at Al Obeid Teaching Hospital since the beginning of the year, illustrating continued pressure on health services despite ongoing conflict.

ICRC documented that cholera-prevention and sanitation activities are being conducted for 6,000 displaced households, with cholera-response materials provided to reinforce outbreak preparedness and health access.

location: Port Sudan to Khartoum humanitarian corridor
notes: ICRC reported that the first RED aircraft flight from Port Sudan to Khartoum since the war began demonstrated critical need for humanitarian air access; aircraft operations shifted to Jeddah base to strengthen humanitarian access.

ICRC reported that 36 trained Red Crescent volunteers are deployed across 12 displacement sites in Al Obeid, providing first aid; between April and May, first-aid teams assisted almost 400 people with injuries and medical needs.
ICRC reported that hundreds of displaced families fled hostilities and violence in Al Fasher, seeking shelter in Tawila town; displacement driven by conflict in North Darfur region.
